top_categories.workout_context: Tour de Brisbane's urban road course on fully closed city streets forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Brisbane's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
68 miles on urban road course on fully closed city streets burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
When someone attacks on urban road course on fully closed city streets, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
60ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour de Brisbane, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on urban road course on fully closed city streets. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for Tour de Brisbane. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
At Tour de Brisbane, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Brisbane. Train it until it feels boring.
The aerobic base that lets you ride urban road course on fully closed city streets at tempo for 68 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,068ft of climbing on urban road course on fully closed city streets does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: April in Brisbane, Queensland, Australia can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Tour de Brisbane. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Tour de Brisbane’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Tour de Brisbane requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on urban road course on fully closed city streets with one significant climb to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 68 miles of urban road course on fully closed city streets with one significant climb in Brisbane, Queensland, Australia.
